seo.title: Building Code Generation with LangGraph + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with LangGraph + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: LangGraph enables building sophisticated code generation agents with stateful, cyclical workflows that generate, test, and refine code iteratively. However, the high-risk nature of executing LLM-generated code introduces severe risks like credential leakage, unsafe dependencies, and infinite loops from cyclic graph failures. Vex provides essential verification layers to ensure code safety, syntax validity, and sandboxed execution before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with LangGraph
architectureOverview.description:
The typical LangGraph code generation workflow starts with a planner node that analyzes requirements and invokes an LLM to generate initial code. This feeds into parallel branches: one for syntax validation and static analysis, another for sandboxed execution with test suite validation. Results merge back into a reviewer node that decides on refinement iterations or final approval, using persistent checkpoints for human-in-the-loop oversight.

State is managed via LangGraph's Checkpoint system, tracking code versions, test results, and execution traces across cycles. Conditional edges handle iteration logic based on test coverage, error rates, and safety scores. The graph supports human breakpoints for manual review of risky code patterns.

Vex integrates at critical junctions: wrapping generated code execution, validating merged state from parallel branches, and checkpoint verification to prevent deserialization failures from schema drift.

risks.name: Cyclic graph execution causing infinite state transitions
risks.description: LLM-generated iteration logic can create infinite loops when test failures trigger endless refinement cycles without convergence criteria.
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors state transition depth and enforces max iterations with automatic termination.

risks.name: State corruption when parallel branches merge conflicting data
risks.description: Syntax validator and sandbox executor produce incompatible code versions that corrupt shared state during merge.
risks.mitigation: Vex validates state schema consistency before checkpoint persistence.

risks.name: Generated code credential leakage
risks.description: LLM hallucinates API keys, database credentials, or secrets in generated code snippets.
risks.mitigation: Vex scans for credential patterns and blocks execution of unsafe code.

risks.name: Unsafe dependency injection
risks.description: Generated code imports malicious packages or executes system commands via pip install.
risks.mitigation: Vex sandbox enforces dependency allowlists and blocks dynamic imports.

implementation.title: Basic LangGraph Code Generation Graph
implementation.description: Core workflow with planner, validator, executor, and reviewer nodes using TypedDict state.
implementation.code:
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END
from langgraph.checkpoint.sqlite import SqliteSaver

import vex

class CodeState(TypedDict):
    requirement: str
    code: str
    syntax_valid: bool
    test_passed: bool
    iteration: Annotated[int, vex.add]
    safety_score: float

async def planner(state: CodeState) -> CodeState:
    # LLM generates initial code
    code = "def hello(): print('Hello World')"  # Replace with LLM call
    return {"code": code, "iteration": 0}

def validator(state: CodeState) -> CodeState:
    try:
        compile(state['code'], '<string>', 'exec')
        return {'syntax_valid': True}
    except:
        return {'syntax_valid': False}

async def executor(state: CodeState) -> CodeState:
    if not state.get('syntax_valid', False):
        return state
    # Sandbox execution
    result = await vex.sandbox.execute(state['code'])
    return {'test_passed': result.passed, 'safety_score': result.safety_score}

def reviewer(state: CodeState) -> str:
    if state.get('test_passed', False) and state['safety_score'] > 0.9:
        return END
    if state['iteration'] > 5:
        return END
    return 'planner'

# Build graph
workflow = StateGraph(CodeState)
workflow.add_node('planner', planner)
workflow.add_node('validator', validator)
workflow.add_node('executor', executor)
workflow.add_node('reviewer', reviewer)

workflow.set_entry_point('planner')
workflow.add_edge('planner', 'validator')
workflow.add_edge('validator', 'executor')
workflow.add_conditional_edges('reviewer', reviewer)

memory = SqliteSaver.from_conn_string(':memory:')
app = workflow.compile(checkpointer=memory)

implementation.title: Vex-Wrapped Graph Invocation
implementation.description: Wrap graph.invoke() with vex.verify() to catch state corruption and infinite loops.

implementation.title: Code Safety Validator Node
implementation.description: Dedicated Vex node for credential scanning and dependency validation.
implementation.code:
async def vex_safety_node(state: CodeState) -> CodeState:
    verification = await vex.verify_code(
        code=state['code'],
        checks=[
            'no_credentials',
            'no_system_calls',
            'dependency_safety',
            'syntax_validity'
        ],
        sandbox=True,
        timeout=30
    )
    
    return {
        'safety_score': verification.score,
        'safety_passed': verification.passed,
        'issues': verification.issues
    }

# Add to graph
workflow.add_node('vex_safety', vex_safety_node)
workflow.add_edge('planner', 'vex_safety')
workflow.add_conditional_edges(
    'vex_safety',
    lambda state: 'executor' if state['safety_passed'] else END
)

implementation.title: Human-in-the-Loop Breakpoint
implementation.description: Implement safe human review with Vex checkpoint validation.

vexIntegration.title: Sandbox Execution Node
vexIntegration.description: Replace raw exec() with Vex sandbox for safe code execution.
vexIntegration.code:
async def vex_sandbox_executor(state: CodeState) -> CodeState:
    sandbox_result = await vex.sandbox.run(
        code=state['code'],
        inputs={'test_data': 'sample_input'},
        timeout=10,
        memory_limit='256MB',
        allowed_packages=['numpy', 'pandas'],  # Dependency safety
        network=False  # No external calls
    )
    
    return {
        'execution_passed': sandbox_result.passed,
        'test_results': sandbox_result.stdout,
        'errors': sandbox_result.stderr,
        'safety_score': sandbox_result.safety
    }

# Integration
workflow.add_node('sandbox_exec', vex_sandbox_executor)
workflow.add_edge('vex_safety', 'sandbox_exec')

vexIntegration.title: State Merge Validator
vexIntegration.description: Prevent corruption when parallel validation branches converge.
vexIntegration.code:
async def vex_state_merger(state: CodeState) -> CodeState:
    merged_state = await vex.verify_state_merge(
        current_state=state,
        expected_schema=CodeState,
        changes=['syntax_valid', 'execution_passed'],
        max_drift=0.1
    )
    
    if not merged_state.valid:
        raise ValueError(f'State corruption detected: {merged_state.errors}')
    
    return {
        **state,
        'merged_at': 'timestamp',
        'drift_score': merged_state.drift
    }

# Add after parallel edges
workflow.add_node('state_merge', vex_state_merger)

vexIntegration.title: Credential Scanner Middleware
vexIntegration.description: Pre-execution scan blocks credential leakage automatically.
vexIntegration.code:
from vex.scanners import CredentialScanner

scanner = CredentialScanner(patterns=['API_KEY', 'PASSWORD', 'SECRET'])

async def credential_guard(state: CodeState) -> CodeState:
    issues = scanner.scan(state['code'])
    
    if issues:
        await vex.log_violation(
            code=state['code'],
            issues=issues,
            context=state['requirement']
        )
        return {'blocked': True, 'issues': issues}
    
    return {'code_cleared': True}

# Wrap planner output
workflow.add_edge('planner', 'credential_guard')
workflow.add_conditional_edges(
    'credential_guard',
    lambda s: END if s.get('blocked') else 'vex_safety'
)

cta.heading: Secure Your LangGraph Code Generation
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your first graph to eliminate code generation risks.
